title: Out-of-scope question injected on a slot turn, invisible to the schema — silently re-asked
defect: Same context-retention defect: an out-of-scope side question with no guard signal and no extractable slot is dropped.
source: Phase 0 multi-intent surface (unsupported question on a delivery confirmation turn)
summary: While confirming the fax number, the member asks an out-of-scope question (copay).
The LLM-1 decode yields no yes/no and no guard (an empty WorkerResult), so the
fax-confirmation branch sees neither a confirmation nor a replacement and simply
re-reads the same fax number. The question is never classified or answered — a soft
silent drop. This is a KNOWN FAILURE: 'must be classified and given a spoken outcome,
never dropped' is not satisfied when the side intent is invisible to the schema.

messages.content: The fax number we have on file is 415-555-3299. Is this correct?

turns.user: Wait — what's the copay for a specialist visit?
turns.note: Empty WorkerResult: no guard, no extracted slot — the question is invisible to the agent.
turns.expected_behavior_target: Classify the out-of-scope question and give a spoken outcome (answer or steer-back), then return to the fax confirmation.
turns.actual_behavior_today: No yes/no detected; the agent re-reads the same fax number and never addresses the copay question.

known_failures.description: An out-of-scope question invisible to the schema is silently re-asked rather than classified and answered.
known_failures.baseline_assertion: After the turn, awaiting_slot=='fax_confirmed' (unchanged), the reply re-reads the fax number, never mentions 'copay', and no provider list is dispatched.
